channel.title: The Grant Desk - Cross-disciplinary & Social Impact, next 90 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Cross-disciplinary & Social Impact, next 90 days.

item.title: Weaving Liberation: Digital Justice Fund (Pilot Round 2026) - Grants between €10,000 and €50,000 per group, to be used over up to two years (2026-2028). Total Fund budget for this first round: €500,000. - deadline 21 Jun 2026
item.description:
Weaving Liberation (resourced by Ford Foundation, Luminate, and Oak Foundation)

Location: Europe (Council of Europe member states; at least 50% of the group must be based in one or multiple Council of Europe countries)

Award: Grants between €10,000 and €50,000 per group, to be used over up to two years (2026-2028). Total Fund budget for this first round: €500,000.

Duration: Up to two years of project work, between 2026 and 2028

Deadline: 21 Jun 2026

Participatory grantmaking fund for not-for-profit groups, collectives and NGOs working on digital justice organising in Europe. Funding decisions are made by a 10-person Peer Circle holding lived knowledge across digital justice, disability justice, workers' rights, racial justice, anti-caste justice, Roma rights, queer and transfeminist justice and other areas; priority to groups led by historically and structurally excluded communities. 'Digital justice organising' is defined as work that builds collective power towards equitable access to resources for all and enables historically and structurally excluded communities to self-determine the purpose, design, production, deployment and governance of digital technologies. What is funded: (1) community building around digital justice (organising, peer-to-peer support, relationship-building); (2) knowledge and capacity building (popular and political education, investigative journalism, skills exchanges, issue mappings of technological harms or community needs, liberatory narratives around tech, artistic interventions, creative approaches); (3) coalition and movement building (advocacy, strategic litigation, campaigning); (4) tech-related mutual aid (repair circles, digital safety/security peer support, shared technical infrastructures, software/hardware sharing); (5) life-affirming technology work (prototyping, piloting or expanding community-centred, transfeminist, anti-colonial technologies). NOT FUNDED: work primarily based or focused outside Europe; large orgs already well-funded by trusts/foundations/HNWIs; intermediary regranters; government-set-up orgs; universities; local branches of well-funded national/international orgs; groups where beneficiaries are not represented in leadership/decision-making; political parties or groups attached to them. Eligibility: based primarily in Europe with activities focused on Europe (coalition work with international groups is welcome); average annual organisational budget over the past 2-3 years not exceeding €500,000; no more than 10 paid staff (long-term freelance contracts count; all-volunteer collectives also welcome); community-led (people directly affected by the issues represented in leadership/decision-making/implementation). NOT open to individuals. Collaborative applications welcome (only the lead org must meet eligibility). Key dates: optional info webinars Wednesday 20 May 2026 at 12:30 CET and Wednesday 27 May 2026 at 16:30 CET (English); platform registration deadline Sunday 24 May 2026, 23:59 CET (registration is required by this date even if application is not yet complete); final application deadline Sunday 21 June 2026, 23:59 CET; shortlisting decisions communicated early August 2026. Submissions via Hypha (open-source submission management, self-hosted on Greenhost VPS in the Netherlands); audio submissions possible via secure transfer in discussion with the team. Application pack available in English, Arabic, Portuguese, Bosnian, German, Spanish, Italian, French (PDF and .docx; only English and Arabic and Bosnian were professionally translated; refer to the English version when in doubt). Free to apply. Contact: digitaljusticefund@weavingliberation.org (Mon-Thu 10-18 CET). Note: this is an ORGANISATIONAL grant for collectives/NGOs, not for individual artists.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: New Profit: Catalyze Connected Futures Cohort 2026 (Discovery Form) - One-year $100,000 unrestricted grant to the organisation, plus a $10,000 leadership development stipend for the Social Entrepreneur, plus strategic advisory support and capacity-building. New Profit covers travel, accommodation and meals for the three in-person convenings. - deadline 26 May 2026
item.description:
New Profit

Location: United States and its territories (organisation must operate in and primarily serve US communities; cohort convenings held in cities across the United States)

Award: One-year $100,000 unrestricted grant to the organisation, plus a $10,000 leadership development stipend for the Social Entrepreneur, plus strategic advisory support and capacity-building. New Profit covers travel, accommodation and meals for the three in-person convenings.

Duration: 12-month cohort experience. Programming requires 6-8 hours per month on average, including three in-person convenings (3-4 days each), three 2-3 hour virtual learning sessions, and a minimum of six 1:1 strategic advising sessions.

Deadline: 26 May 2026

New Catalyze cohort from venture philanthropy organisation New Profit, supporting US-based non-profits whose core work is bridging active divides (political, racial, ethnic, economic, religious, geographic, generational, or ideological) to enable collective problem-solving in education, economic mobility, democracy, and/or health. Funded organisations must use one or more of: building skills for productive dialogue, conflict, and civic participation; cultivating relationships rooted in trust, understanding, belonging, and mutual accountability; or creating structures for collective action, co-design, problem-solving, or shared decision-making. Organisations receive a one-year $100,000 unrestricted grant, a $10,000 leadership development stipend, and a year of cohort-based capacity building (three in-person convenings, three virtual learning sessions, 1:1 strategic advising). Eligibility: US-focused; independent 501(c)(3) status (own or via fiscal sponsor); annual expenses $250,000-$2 million in most recently closed fiscal year; led by at least one full-time (~30 hrs/week) Social Entrepreneur (co-leadership models considered); core programme/impact approach in operation for at least two years (legal status timeline less important); not a previous New Profit Build or Catalyze grantee. The first step is a Discovery Form (NOT a full application). To be considered for this cycle, complete the Discovery Form by Tuesday 26 May 2026, 2pm PT / 5pm ET, selecting 'Cycle-specific submission' and 'Connected Futures 2026'. If you submitted a Discovery Form within the last 12 months, email selection@newprofit.org for a link to update your prior submission. Invitations to complete a full application will be sent in late June. Discovery Forms are also accepted on a rolling basis for grantmaking cycles in 2026 and beyond. Free to apply. Note: this is an organisational grant, not an individual grant.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Creative Industry Activities Programme Grant Scheme - Up to €125,000 per year per applicant (overall budget €1,850,000 per year) - deadline 15 Jun 2026
item.description:
Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie (Creative Industries Fund NL)

Location: Netherlands

Award: Up to €125,000 per year per applicant (overall budget €1,850,000 per year)

Duration: Two-year activities programme

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

Multi-year grant for cultural institutions whose core task is to contribute to the high quality, development and professionalisation of the contemporary creative industry through a two-year activities programme. Application window: 13 May 2026 15:00 CEST to 15 June 2026 16:00 CEST. Maximum requested amount €125,000 per year; total annual scheme budget €1,850,000.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: International Vouchers - Up to €1,500 within Europe (zone 1) / up to €2,500 outside Europe (zone 2) - deadline 22 Jun 2026
item.description:
Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie (Creative Industries Fund NL)

Location: Netherlands (for international travel)

Award: Up to €1,500 within Europe (zone 1) / up to €2,500 outside Europe (zone 2)

Duration: Per trip

Deadline: 22 Jun 2026

Travel voucher for Dutch-based professionals in design, architecture and/or digital culture who have been invited by a foreign party to give a presentation, lecture or workshop. To apply you must have submitted an application to the Fund in the past five years that was positively assessed. Round 2 runs 4 May 2026 15:00 CEST to 22 June 2026 16:00 CEST (budget €33,000); Round 3 runs 8 September 2026 15:00 CEST to 27 October 2026 16:00 CEST (budget €33,000).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Next Generation Foresight Practitioners (NGFP) Fellowship 2027 - $1,000 kick-starter grant + mentorship + global networking + opportunity to win $10,000 grand prize at year-end - deadline 26 Jun 2026
item.description:
Next Generation Foresight Practitioners (NGFP)

Location: Global (online community plus regional hubs)

Award: $1,000 kick-starter grant + mentorship + global networking + opportunity to win $10,000 grand prize at year-end

Duration: 1 year

Deadline: 26 Jun 2026

Fellowship for emerging changemakers aged 18 to 35 using futures thinking and foresight to drive social and environmental impact. Fellows receive a $1,000 kick-starter grant, mentorship from expert foresight practitioners, capability-building training, access to a 900+ strong global community, regional hubs, and the chance to win a $10,000 grand prize. Supports projects on climate transitions, democracy, emerging technologies, health and other systemic challenges.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
